title: $2.5B Amazon Prime FTC Settlement - Dark Pattern Enrollment
eligibility: Consumers who signed up for Amazon Prime between June 23, 2019 and June 23, 2025 through a challenged enrollment flow, or who attempted to cancel but were unable to do so.
eligibilityBullets: Signed up for Amazon Prime between June 2019 and June 2025
Enrolled through a challenged enrollment flow (e.g., shipping page, single-page checkout)
Used fewer than 10 Prime benefits in any 12-month period, or attempted to cancel unsuccessfully
summary: Amazon agreed to pay $2.5 billion to settle FTC allegations that it used dark patterns to trick millions of consumers into enrolling in Prime subscriptions and made cancellation unnecessarily difficult.
caseInfo: FTC v. Amazon.com Inc., Case No. 2:23-cv-00932-JHC

title: $60.5M Tinder Age Discrimination Pricing Settlement
eligibility: Consumers who purchased Tinder Plus or Tinder Gold in California when over age 29 (on or after March 2, 2015) or over age 28 (on or after March 2, 2016) and were charged a higher price due to their age.
eligibilityBullets: Purchased Tinder Plus or Tinder Gold in California
Were over age 29 (from March 2015) or over age 28 (from March 2016)
Were charged a higher subscription price due to age
proofDetails: If you did not receive a class notice, provide your name, contact info, approximate purchase date, state, date of birth or age, and estimated total amount paid with supporting documentation.
summary: Tinder agreed to pay $60.5 million to resolve claims it charged older users more for Tinder Plus and Gold subscriptions in California, violating the Unruh Civil Rights Act.
caseInfo: Candelore v. Tinder, Inc., Case No. BC583162, Superior Court of California, Los Angeles

title: $90M Joint Juice Glucosamine False Health Claims Settlement
eligibility: Consumers who purchased Joint Juice glucosamine supplements in California, Connecticut, Florida, Illinois, Maryland, Massachusetts, Michigan, Pennsylvania, or New York.
eligibilityBullets: Purchased Joint Juice glucosamine supplements
Resided in CA, CT, FL, IL, MD, MA, MI, PA, or NY at time of purchase
Purchase was during the applicable class period for your state
summary: Joint Juice agreed to pay $90 million total to settle claims it falsely advertised its glucosamine supplements' health benefits. Combined multi-state ($70M) and New York ($19.16M) settlements.
scope: Multi-state (CA, CT, FL, IL, MD, MA, MI, PA, NY)
caseInfo: Joint Juice False Advertising Litigation

title: $100M Walmart Spark Driver Pay & Misclassification Settlement
eligibility: Walmart Spark delivery drivers who lost earnings due to deceptive pay representations or withheld tips.
eligibilityBullets: Worked as a Walmart Spark delivery driver
Experienced deceptive earnings claims or withheld tips
Drove for Spark during the relevant time period
proofDetails: Automatic payment to identified Spark drivers - no claim form
summary: Walmart agreed to pay $100 million to settle FTC and state charges that it misled Spark delivery drivers about earnings and withheld tips from gig workers.
caseInfo: FTC v. Walmart Inc.

title: $14M Copa America Final Unsafe Stadium Crowd Conditions Settlement
eligibility: Valid ticketholders to the Copa America Final on July 14, 2024 at Hard Rock Stadium who were denied entry or admitted but denied full access to their purchased seats.
eligibilityBullets: Held a valid ticket to the Copa America Final on July 14, 2024
Were denied entry to Hard Rock Stadium, OR
Were admitted but denied full access to facilities or purchased seats
proofDetails: Valid ticket and notice ID/PIN from settlement notification. Denied entry claims may require photo/video evidence.
summary: A $14 million settlement resolves claims that ticketholders to the 2024 Copa America Final were wrongfully denied entry or full access at Hard Rock Stadium in Miami. Up to $2,000 for denied entry, up to $100 for limited access.
caseInfo: Copa America Final Class Action, Miami

title: $5M Sprouts Farmers Market Printed Receipt Violation Settlement
eligibility: Consumers who used a credit or debit card at any Sprouts store between August 16, 2020 and October 31, 2022, or an EBT card between March 15, 2021 and April 15, 2023, and received a receipt showing more than the last 5 digits of their card number.
eligibilityBullets: Used a credit/debit card at Sprouts (Aug 2020 - Oct 2022) or EBT card (Mar 2021 - Apr 2023)
Received a receipt showing more than the last 5 digits of your card number
U.S. resident
proofDetails: If you received a notice with a claim ID, use the short-form claim. Otherwise, submit a long-form claim with a receipt or card statement.
summary: Sprouts agreed to pay $5 million to settle claims it printed more than the last five digits of customer card numbers on receipts, violating the Fair and Accurate Credit Transactions Act (FACTA).
caseInfo: Tran, et al. v. Sprouts Farmers Market Inc., Case No. 22STCV26572

title: $11.5M Cosequin Dog Joint Supplement Mislabeling Settlement
eligibility: California residents who purchased Cosequin canine joint supplements for personal use between May 3, 2016 and May 6, 2022.
eligibilityBullets: Reside (or resided) in California
Purchased Cosequin dog joint supplements for personal use
Purchase was between May 3, 2016 and May 6, 2022
summary: Nutramax agreed to pay $11.5 million over claims it falsely marketed Cosequin dog supplements. No proof of purchase required. Up to $25 per unit purchased, max $150 per household.
caseInfo: Cosequin Dog Supplement Class Action, California

title: $200.2M Beef & Pork Worker Wage-Fixing Conspiracy Settlement
eligibility: Employees who worked at a beef or pork processing plant for JBS, Tyson, Cargill, Hormel, National Beef, Seaboard, Smithfield, or Perdue between January 1, 2000 and February 27, 2024.
eligibilityBullets: Worked at a beef or pork processing plant in the continental U.S.
Employed by JBS, Tyson, Cargill, Hormel, National Beef, or other defendants
Employment was between January 2000 and February 2024
proofDetails: Employment and earnings information at a qualifying beef or pork processing plant. If you received a notice, update your employment and earnings info. If not, submit a Participation Form with employment details.
summary: Nine settlements totaling $200.2 million resolve claims that major meat processors conspired to suppress worker wages through secret compensation meetings and data sharing.
caseInfo: Brown et al. v. JBS USA Food Company et al., Case No. 1:22-cv-02946

title: $17.5M American Express Merchant Fee Antitrust Settlement
eligibility: Consumers who used a nonrewards Visa, Mastercard, or Discover credit card at qualifying retailers in Illinois between 2015 and 2022.
eligibilityBullets: Used a non-rewards Visa, Mastercard, or Discover credit card
Made purchases at qualifying Illinois retailers (Walmart, Kroger, Target, etc.)
Purchases were made between 2015 and 2022
summary: American Express agreed to pay $17.5 million to settle antitrust claims that it harmed consumers who used competing non-rewards credit cards at 38 major retailers in Illinois.
caseInfo: American Express Antitrust Litigation, Illinois

title: $299.5M Toyota Forklift Harmful Emissions Exposure Settlement
eligibility: Individuals or businesses that purchased or leased a Toyota internal combustion (IC) forklift built between 2007 and 2021.
eligibilityBullets: Purchased or leased a Toyota IC forklift
Forklift was built between 2007 and 2021
Located in the United States
proofDetails: Forklift model number, serial number, and proof of ownership or lease (title, registration, purchase/lease agreement, or proof of insurance).
summary: Toyota agreed to a $299.5 million settlement over claims that its IC forklifts did not meet emissions standards. Qualifying owners can receive $1,000-$2,500 per forklift plus a free service visit.
caseInfo: Toyota Forklift Emissions Class Action

title: $5.25M Cadence Bank MOVEit Data Breach & Privacy Settlement
eligibility: Individuals whom Cadence Bank notified about the MOVEit data breach that compromised their personal information.
eligibilityBullets: Received a data breach notification from Cadence Bank
Personal information was compromised in the MOVEit data breach
U.S. resident
proofDetails: Unique ID and PIN from settlement notice required to file a claim.
summary: Cadence Bank agreed to a $5.25 million settlement to resolve claims related to the MOVEit file transfer data breach. Eligible class members can claim up to $12,500.
caseInfo: Cadence Bank MOVEit Data Breach Litigation

title: $4.17M RevitaLash Eye Serum Deceptive Marketing Settlement
individualPayout: Up to $110 voucher or cash payment
eligibility: Consumers who purchased RevitaLash or RevitaBrow lash and brow serum products in the U.S. from January 1, 2017 through December 29, 2025.
eligibilityBullets: Purchased RevitaLash or RevitaBrow products in the U.S.
Purchase made between January 1, 2017 and December 29, 2025
Can file with or without proof of purchase
proofDetails: No proof required for voucher credit. Proof of purchase or matching Athena records needed for higher cash payment.
summary: Athena Cosmetics agreed to pay $4.17 million to settle claims that its RevitaLash and RevitaBrow products contain potentially dangerous ingredients that were not disclosed on labeling.
caseInfo: Athena Cosmetics RevitaLash Settlement

title: $3.5M Ideal Image Website Tracking & Privacy Violation Settlement
eligibility: Individuals in the U.S. who used idealimage.com to schedule a consultation for services between January 1, 2023 and January 26, 2026.
eligibilityBullets: Used idealimage.com to schedule a consultation
Consultation scheduled between January 1, 2023 and January 26, 2026
Must have Facebook account with matching name
proofDetails: Requires proof of Facebook account with name matching the claim form. Online claims need class member ID from settlement notice.
summary: Ideal Image agreed to pay $3.5 million to settle claims that it shared users' personally identifiable information with Facebook through tracking technologies on its website without consent.
caseInfo: Gayle Minano v. Ideal Image Development Corp., Case No. 23-CA-014439

title: $87.5M Tyson & Cargill Beef Price-Fixing Consumer Settlement
eligibility: Consumers who indirectly purchased beef from chuck, loin, rib or round primal cuts in eligible states between August 1, 2014 and December 31, 2019.
eligibilityBullets: Bought beef (fresh or frozen, chuck/loin/rib/round cuts) at a grocery store
Purchased between August 1, 2014 and December 31, 2019
Purchased in AZ, CA, DC, FL, IL, IA, KS, MA, ME, MI, MN, MO, MT, NE, NV, NH, NM, NY, NC, ND, OR, RI, SD, TN, UT, WV, or WI
proofDetails: No receipts required at filing, but the administrator may request verification later.
summary: Tyson ($55M) and Cargill ($32.5M) agreed to pay $87.5 million to settle allegations that major beef processors conspired to fix prices and reduce supply, causing consumers to overpay for beef.
caseInfo: In re: Cattle and Beef Antitrust Litigation, Case No. 0:22-MD-3031

title: Shimano Hollowtech II Crankset Safety Recall & Replacement Settlement
eligibility: Individuals or businesses who owned a Shimano Ultegra or Dura-Ace crankset manufactured before July 2019 or a bicycle equipped with one in the United States.
eligibilityBullets: Owned a Shimano Ultegra FC-6800/R8000 or Dura-Ace FC-9000/R9100/R9100-P crankset
Crankset manufactured before July 2019 with eligible production code
Replaced the crankset due to separation or delamination issues
proofDetails: Proof of purchase and replacement costs required. Declaration under penalty of perjury accepted if documentation unavailable.
summary: Shimano settled a class action after recalling approximately 760,000 Ultegra and Dura-Ace cranksets due to bonding separation and delamination issues. Benefits include extended warranty, inspections, and cost reimbursement.
caseInfo: In re Shimano Crankset Litigation, Case No. 8:23-cv-02038-JVS-JDE

title: $212K Hefty Recyclable Bags False Green Advertising Settlement
eligibility: Arizona consumers who purchased Reynolds' Recycling bags or transparent blue or clear Hefty bags.
eligibilityBullets: Purchased Reynolds Recycling bags or Hefty transparent blue/clear bags
Must be an Arizona resident
File a complaint with the Attorney General's Office
summary: Reynolds Consumer Products agreed to pay $212,000 to resolve allegations that Hefty Recycling bags were misleadingly marketed as recyclable when not accepted by most recycling programs.
caseInfo: State of Arizona v. Reynolds Consumer Products Inc., et al.

title: $630M Google Play Store App Developer Antitrust Overcharge Settlement
eligibility: Consumers in the U.S., Puerto Rico, or U.S. Virgin Islands who purchased an app or paid for in-app content through Google Play Billing between August 16, 2016 and September 30, 2023.
eligibilityBullets: Purchased apps or in-app content through Google Play Store
Purchases made between August 16, 2016 and September 30, 2023
Most people will receive automatic payment via PayPal or Venmo
proofDetails: Automatic payment to identified Google Play purchasers
summary: Google agreed to pay $630 million to settle allegations that its Google Play Store engaged in anticompetitive practices by charging excessive fees and restricting app distribution, resulting in higher prices for consumers.
scope: Nationwide (including Puerto Rico and U.S. Virgin Islands)
caseInfo: State of Utah et al. v. Google LLC et al., Case No. 3:21-cv-05227-JD

title: $65M Robinhood Order Flow Settlement
eligibility: Robinhood customers who placed certain qualifying trades between September 1, 2016 and September 1, 2018.
eligibilityBullets: Had a Robinhood account
Placed qualifying trades between September 1, 2016 and September 1, 2018
No proof of purchase required
proofDetails: Active Robinhood account for automatic payment, or submit claim form with account details for those without active account.
summary: Robinhood agreed to settle claims that it failed to disclose its payment-for-order-flow practices and did not seek the best execution for customer trades. Eligible customers who traded during the class period can claim a share.
caseInfo: Robinhood Order Flow Settlement

title: Grubhub & Seamless Delivery Fee Settlement - $10 Credit
eligibility: Consumers who made a food delivery order and paid through the Grubhub or Seamless app or website between January 24, 2019 and January 12, 2026 to a California address.
eligibilityBullets: Ordered food delivery via Grubhub or Seamless
Order was delivered to a California address
Ordered between January 24, 2019 and January 12, 2026
proofDetails: Unique ID from email notice required to file claim
summary: Grubhub agreed to settle claims related to its delivery fee practices in California. Eligible class members can receive a $10 credit toward a future Grubhub or Seamless order.
caseInfo: Grubhub Delivery Fees Settlement

title: Christian Dior Data Breach Settlement - Up to $1,600 + Monitoring
eligibility: Individuals who were notified that their personal information may have been accessed in the January 2025 Christian Dior data breach.
eligibilityBullets: Received a data breach notification from Christian Dior
Personal information was accessed in the January 2025 breach
No proof of purchase required
proofDetails: Must have received data breach notification from Christian Dior. Login credentials from notice required.
summary: Christian Dior agreed to settle claims related to a January 2025 data breach that exposed customers' personal information. Class members can receive up to $1,600 in cash and free credit monitoring services.
caseInfo: Christian Dior Data Breach Settlement

title: Norton Healthcare Data Breach Settlement - $5 to $2,585
eligibility: Individuals who were notified that their personal information may have been exposed in the May 2023 Norton Healthcare data breach.
eligibilityBullets: Received a data breach notification from Norton Healthcare
Personal information was exposed in the May 2023 breach
No proof required to file a claim
proofDetails: Must have received data breach notification from Norton Healthcare.
summary: Norton Healthcare agreed to settle claims related to a May 2023 data breach that exposed patient information. Affected individuals can receive between $5 and $2,585.
caseInfo: Norton Healthcare Data Breach Settlement

title: PharMerica Data Breach Settlement - Up to $10,000+
eligibility: Individuals who received notice stating that their personal information was exposed in the March 2023 PharMerica data breach.
eligibilityBullets: Received a data breach notice from PharMerica
Personal information was exposed in the March 2023 breach
No proof required to file a basic claim
proofDetails: Must have received data breach notification from PharMerica.
summary: PharMerica agreed to settle claims related to a March 2023 data breach affecting patient data. Payouts can reach $10,000 or more depending on documented out-of-pocket losses related to the breach.
caseInfo: PharMerica Data Breach Settlement

title: Sealy Bedding Thread Count Settlement - $5 to $40+
eligibility: Consumers who bought one or more bedding products with the Sealy name that had a listed thread count of 1250 on the packaging.
eligibilityBullets: Purchased Sealy-branded bedding products
Product packaging listed thread count of 1250
No proof of purchase required
summary: Sealy agreed to settle claims that it misrepresented the thread count on certain bedding products. Consumers who bought Sealy products with a listed thread count of 1250 can receive $5 to $40+ per product.
caseInfo: Sealy Bedding Thread Count Settlement

title: Stoner Cats NFT SEC Fair Fund Settlement
eligibility: Individuals who purchased Stoner Cats NFTs from Stoner Cats 2, LLC in the offering on July 27, 2021.
eligibilityBullets: Purchased Stoner Cats NFTs during the July 27, 2021 offering
Purchased directly from Stoner Cats 2, LLC
Proof of purchase required
proofDetails: Proof of NFT purchase from the July 27, 2021 offering, such as wallet transaction records.
summary: The SEC established a Fair Fund for investors who purchased Stoner Cats NFTs in the July 2021 offering. The project was found to have offered unregistered securities.
caseInfo: Stoner Cats NFT SEC Fair Fund

title: Boostrix Vaccination (New York) Settlement - $10 to $50
eligibility: New York residents who were vaccinated with Boostrix or received the vaccination in the state between May 20, 2016 and May 20, 2020 after viewing certain advertisements about the vaccine.
eligibilityBullets: Lived in New York or received Boostrix in New York
Vaccinated between May 20, 2016 and May 20, 2020
Viewed Boostrix advertisements before vaccination
summary: GlaxoSmithKline agreed to settle claims related to advertising of its Boostrix vaccine in New York. Eligible individuals who were vaccinated after viewing certain ads can receive $10 to $50.
caseInfo: Boostrix Vaccination (New York) Settlement

title: Hyundai & Kia Vehicle Theft Settlement - $375 to $4,500
eligibility: Owners or lessees of certain 2011-2022 Hyundai or Kia vehicles that were stolen or nearly stolen on or after April 29, 2025.
eligibilityBullets: Owned or leased a 2011-2022 Hyundai or Kia vehicle
Vehicle was stolen or nearly stolen on or after April 29, 2025
Proof of theft or attempted theft required
proofDetails: Proof of vehicle ownership/lease and documentation of theft or attempted theft (police report, insurance claim, etc.).
summary: Hyundai and Kia agreed to settle claims related to the widespread theft vulnerability in certain 2011-2022 vehicles that lacked engine immobilizers. Stolen vehicle owners can receive $375 to $4,500.
caseInfo: Hyundai/Kia Vehicle Theft Multi-State Immobilizer Settlement

title: Mitsubishi Airbag Control Units Settlement - Up to $250
eligibility: Individuals who owned or leased certain Mitsubishi Lancer or Outlander vehicles as of November 1, 2024.
eligibilityBullets: Owned or leased a Mitsubishi Lancer or Outlander
Ownership/lease as of November 1, 2024
Vehicle must be on the list of qualifying models
proofDetails: Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) and proof of ownership or lease.
summary: Mitsubishi agreed to settle claims related to defective airbag control units in certain Lancer and Outlander vehicles. Eligible owners and lessees can receive up to $250.
caseInfo: Mitsubishi Airbag Control Units Settlement

title: Westlake Financial Services Payment Fees Settlement
eligibility: Individuals who were charged a fee for making a payment on a Westlake Services account through ACI payments between June 20, 2022 and August 18, 2025.
eligibilityBullets: Had a Westlake Services account
Charged a payment processing fee through ACI
Fee was charged between June 20, 2022 and August 18, 2025
proofDetails: Unique ID and PIN from settlement notice required
summary: Westlake Financial Services agreed to settle claims related to payment processing fees charged to borrowers through ACI payments. Eligible claimants can receive a refund of fees paid.
caseInfo: Westlake Financial Services Fees Settlement

title: Mercedes-Benz Emissions Warranty Settlement
eligibility: Individuals who paid out of pocket to diagnose, repair, or replace covered vehicle parts for a model year 2015 to present Mercedes-Benz vehicle between 4 years/50,000 miles and 7 years/70,000 miles.
eligibilityBullets: Own or owned a 2015 or newer Mercedes-Benz vehicle
Paid out of pocket for diagnosis, repair, or replacement of covered parts
Repair occurred between 4 years/50K miles and 7 years/70K miles
proofDetails: Repair receipts or invoices showing out-of-pocket payments for covered emissions-related parts.
summary: Mercedes-Benz agreed to settle claims related to emissions warranty coverage. Owners who paid out of pocket for covered repairs after the standard warranty but within the emissions warranty period can seek reimbursement.
caseInfo: Mercedes-Benz Emissions Warranty Settlement

title: Dodge Ram EcoDiesel EGR Cooler Settlement
eligibility: Individuals who bought or leased a 2014-2019 Dodge Ram 1500 EcoDiesel truck manufactured between June 12, 2013 and October 23, 2019.
eligibilityBullets: Bought or leased a 2014-2019 Dodge Ram 1500 EcoDiesel
Vehicle was manufactured between June 12, 2013 and October 23, 2019
Proof of ownership/lease and repair records required
proofDetails: Proof of ownership or lease and documentation of EGR cooler-related repairs or issues.
summary: Stellantis (formerly FCA) agreed to settle claims related to defective EGR coolers in 2014-2019 Dodge Ram 1500 EcoDiesel trucks. Owners and lessees can seek compensation for repairs and related expenses.
caseInfo: Dodge Ram EcoDiesel EGR Cooler Settlement

title: Toyota Camry HVAC (California) Settlement
eligibility: Current and former 2012-2015 Toyota Camry XV50 owners and lessees in California.
eligibilityBullets: Owned or leased a 2012-2015 Toyota Camry XV50
Located in California
Proof of ownership/lease required
proofDetails: Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) and proof of ownership or lease in California.
summary: Toyota agreed to settle claims related to HVAC system defects in 2012-2015 Camry XV50 models sold in California. Eligible owners and lessees can receive compensation for repairs.
caseInfo: Toyota Camry HVAC (California) Settlement

title: Ford PowerShift Transmission Settlement
eligibility: Current and former owners and lessees of 2011-2016 Ford Fiesta and 2012-2016 Ford Focus vehicles who had issues with their PowerShift transmissions.
eligibilityBullets: Owned or leased a 2011-2016 Ford Fiesta or 2012-2016 Ford Focus
Experienced issues with the PowerShift transmission
Proof of ownership and repair records required
proofDetails: Proof of ownership or lease and documentation of PowerShift transmission issues or repairs.
summary: Ford agreed to settle claims related to defective PowerShift dual-clutch transmissions in Fiesta and Focus models. The settlement provides compensation for repairs, buybacks, and related expenses. Claims accepted on a rolling basis.
caseInfo: Ford PowerShift Transmissions Settlement

title: Boohoo Advertised Sales Settlement - $10 Gift Card
eligibility: Consumers who bought products from Boohoo, BoohooMAN, PrettyLittleThing, or Nasty Gal outside of California between April 1, 2016 and June 17, 2022.
eligibilityBullets: Purchased from Boohoo, BoohooMAN, PrettyLittleThing, or Nasty Gal
Purchase was outside of California
Purchased between April 1, 2016 and June 17, 2022
proofDetails: Automatic gift cards to identified purchasers - no claim form
summary: Boohoo Group agreed to settle claims related to deceptive pricing practices on Boohoo, BoohooMAN, PrettyLittleThing, and Nasty Gal websites. Eligible consumers can receive a $10 gift card. Claims accepted on a rolling basis.
caseInfo: Boohoo Advertised Sales Settlement

title: Traeger Pellet Grills (California, Utah) Settlement - $3 Coupon
eligibility: Consumers who bought wood pellets made by Traeger Pellet Grills in California or Utah after October 1, 2015.
eligibilityBullets: Purchased Traeger wood pellets
Purchased in California or Utah
Purchased after October 1, 2015
summary: Traeger agreed to settle claims related to the labeling and marketing of its wood pellets in California and Utah. Eligible purchasers can receive a $3 coupon.
caseInfo: Traeger Pellet Grills (California, Utah) Settlement

title: Avanquest Software Subscription Fees Settlement
eligibility: Individuals who were enrolled in a subscription for an Avanquest Software product and charged a subscription fee between November 16, 2019 and August 31, 2025.
eligibilityBullets: Had an Avanquest Software subscription
Were charged a subscription fee
Charges occurred between November 16, 2019 and August 31, 2025
proofDetails: WebID from settlement notice required
summary: Avanquest Software agreed to settle claims related to unauthorized or undisclosed subscription fee charges. Eligible subscribers can receive a refund of fees charged during the class period.
caseInfo: Avanquest Software Subscription Fees Settlement

title: McLaren Health Care Data Breach Settlement - Up to $5,000
eligibility: Individuals whose private information was compromised as a result of data breaches at McLaren Health Care Corporation in 2023 or 2024.
eligibilityBullets: Private information was compromised in McLaren data breach
Data breach occurred in 2023 or 2024
No proof required to file a basic claim
proofDetails: Must have received data breach notification from McLaren Health Care.
summary: McLaren Health Care agreed to settle claims related to data breaches in 2023 and 2024 that compromised patient information. Affected individuals can receive up to $5,000 based on documented losses.
caseInfo: McLaren Health Care Data Breaches Settlement

title: Sutter Health Data Privacy (California) Settlement - Up to $90
eligibility: California residents who logged into their Sutter Health MyHealthOnline portal account between June 10, 2015 and March 20, 2020.
eligibilityBullets: California resident
Logged into Sutter Health MyHealthOnline portal
Used the portal between June 10, 2015 and March 20, 2020
proofDetails: Unique ID from settlement notice required
summary: Sutter Health agreed to settle claims that it shared patient data from its MyHealthOnline portal with third-party analytics tools without proper consent. California residents who used the portal can receive up to $90.
caseInfo: Sutter Health Data Privacy (California) Settlement

title: Northwell Health Data Privacy Settlement - $15
eligibility: Northwell patients who logged into Northwell's FollowMyHealth patient portal or booked an appointment on Northwell's website between January 1, 2020 and December 31, 2023, or who were Northwell patients between January 1, 2020 and July 25, 2024.
eligibilityBullets: Were a Northwell Health patient
Logged into FollowMyHealth portal or booked online between January 2020 and December 2023
Or were a Northwell patient between January 2020 and July 2024
proofDetails: Notice ID and PIN from settlement notice required
summary: Northwell Health agreed to settle claims that it used tracking pixels on its website and patient portal that shared patient data with third parties without consent. Eligible patients can receive $15.
caseInfo: Northwell Health Data Privacy Settlement

title: Centrelake Medical Group Data Breach (CA) Settlement - $20 to $3,500
eligibility: Centrelake Medical Group patients with a California address who received a data breach notice in April 2019.
eligibilityBullets: Were a Centrelake Medical Group patient
Had a California address
Received a data breach notice in April 2019
proofDetails: Must have received data breach notification from Centrelake Medical Group.
summary: Centrelake Medical Group agreed to settle claims related to a data breach that exposed patient information. California patients who received notice can receive $20 to $3,500 based on documented losses.
caseInfo: Centrelake Medical Group Data Breach (California) Settlement

title: Tangoe US Data Breach Settlement - $50 to $5,750
eligibility: Individuals whose private information was compromised in the November 2022 Tangoe US data breach.
eligibilityBullets: Private information was compromised in the Tangoe data breach
Data breach occurred in November 2022
No proof required to file a basic claim
proofDetails: Must have received data breach notification from Tangoe US.
summary: Tangoe US agreed to settle claims related to a November 2022 data breach that exposed personal information. Affected individuals can receive between $50 and $5,750 depending on documented losses.
caseInfo: Tangoe US Data Breach Settlement

title: $4M Essen Medical Associates data breach class action settlement
eligibility: Individuals whose personal information was compromised in the Essen Medical Associates data breach between March 14 and March 22, 2023.
eligibilityBullets: Personal information was compromised in the Essen Medical data breach
Data breach occurred between March 14 and March 22, 2023
Received a data breach notification from Essen Medical
proofDetails: Documentation may be required to verify your claim.
summary: Essen Medical Associates agreed to pay $4 million to settle claims that it failed to prevent a data breach in March 2023 that compromised sensitive consumer information including names, Social Security numbers, and medical records.

title: $117.5M Comcast Data Breach Settlement
eligibility: Consumers who received a data breach notification from Comcast about the Oct. 2023 breach
eligibilityBullets: Received a data breach notice from Comcast
Information was compromised between Oct. 16-19, 2023
Comcast publicly disclosed the breach in Dec. 2023
proofDetails: You need your Unique ID from the breach notification letter (or look it up on ComcastBreachSettlement.com). No receipts needed for the $50 flat payment. Documentation required for up to $10,000 in out-of-pocket losses.
summary: Comcast agreed to pay $117.5 million after a 2023 data breach compromised customer information. You can claim $50 with no proof, or up to $10,000 for documented losses like identity theft or credit monitoring costs. Also includes 3 years of free identity protection services.
caseInfo: Hasson v. Comcast Cable Communications LLC, Case No. 2:23-cv-05039-JMY, U.S. District Court for the Eastern District of Pennsylvania

title: $2.9M Tom's of Maine Toothpaste Contamination Settlement
eligibility: Anyone who purchased Tom's of Maine toothpaste between Nov. 2020 and March 2026
eligibilityBullets: Purchased any Tom's of Maine brand toothpaste
Purchase was between Nov. 21, 2020 and March 6, 2026
No proof of purchase required (but receipts increase payout)
proofDetails: No proof of purchase required. With a receipt, you can get a full refund for up to 3 products.
summary: Colgate-Palmolive is paying $2.9 million after an FDA inspection found Tom's of Maine toothpaste failed manufacturing standards. If you bought any Tom's of Maine toothpaste since Nov. 2020, you can get a cash refund - no receipt needed.
caseInfo: Rabinowitz, et al. v. Colgate-Palmolive Co., et al., Case No. 2:25-cv-06996-JMW, U.S. District Court for the Eastern District of New York

title: $24.75M Grubhub Driver Misclassification Settlement
eligibility: Grubhub delivery drivers who completed at least one delivery in California between Dec. 2014 and March 2026
eligibilityBullets: Used the Grubhub platform as an independent contractor
Accepted or completed at least one delivery in California
Drove for Grubhub between Dec. 3, 2014 and March 13, 2026
proofDetails: You must have been a Grubhub delivery driver in California. Grubhub has records of who drove for them - the settlement admin will verify your identity.
summary: Grubhub is paying $24.75 million to settle claims it misclassified California delivery drivers as independent contractors instead of employees. If you made any Grubhub deliveries in California, you can claim at least $25 - more if you drove more miles.
caseInfo: Lawson, et al. v. Grubhub Holdings Inc., et al., Case No. 15-cv-05128-JSC, U.S. District Court for the Northern District of California

title: $3.85M Cardiovascular Consultants Data Breach Settlement
eligibility: Individuals whose personal information was compromised in the Sept. 2023 Cardiovascular Consultants data breach
eligibilityBullets: Your personal info was in the CVC data breach (Sept. 2023)
You received a data breach notification from CVC
Includes names, SSNs, medical info, and driver's license numbers
proofDetails: You need to be identified as someone whose info was compromised in the Sept. 2023 CVC data breach. No receipts needed for the $75 pro rata payment. Documentation required for up to $5,000 in out-of-pocket losses.
summary: Cardiovascular Consultants is paying $3.85 million after a 2023 data breach exposed patient names, Social Security numbers, and medical records. You can claim $75 with no proof, or up to $5,000 for documented losses. Also includes 2 years of medical monitoring.
caseInfo: Stroup, et al. v. Cardiovascular Consultants Ltd., Case No. CV2023-020048, Superior Court of Arizona, Maricopa County

title: Differin BPO Acne Products Benzene Contamination Settlement - Up to $27
eligibility: Consumers who purchased Differin BPO acne products (not regular Adapalene gel) between January 1, 2020 and February 19, 2026 in the United States.
eligibilityBullets: Purchased Differin Daily Deep Cleanser (5% BPO), Acne Spot Treatment (10% BPO), or Max Strength Foaming Cleanser (10% BPO)
Purchased between January 2020 and February 2026
Does NOT include regular Differin Adapalene Gel 0.1%
summary: Galderma agreed to settle claims that certain Differin BPO (benzoyl peroxide) acne products contained elevated levels of benzene. Claim $9 per product for up to 3 products with no proof of purchase - just attest under penalty of perjury. Only covers BPO products, not the popular Differin Adapalene Gel.
caseInfo: Williams v. Galderma Laboratories, L.P., Case No. 1:24-cv-02222, N.D. Illinois

title: $17.25M PowerSchool Naviance Student Data Tracking Settlement
individualPayout: Pro rata share (depends on claims filed)
eligibility: All U.S. students who logged into Naviance at any point between August 18, 2021 and January 23, 2026.
eligibilityBullets: Logged into Naviance (college/career planning tool) at least once
Between August 18, 2021 and January 23, 2026
Were a student at the time of use
summary: PowerSchool agreed to pay $17.25M to settle claims it tracked student data through its Naviance college planning platform. If you used Naviance as a student, you can file a claim for a pro rata cash payment - no proof of purchase needed, just your name and the email you used for Naviance.
caseInfo: Q.J. v. PowerSchool Holdings LLC, et al.

title: Hyundai & Kia Airbag Control Unit Settlement - $150 to $350
eligibility: Owners or lessees of certain Hyundai and Kia vehicles with ZF-TRW airbag control unit defects.
eligibilityBullets: Own or lease a Hyundai or Kia with ZF-TRW airbag control unit
VIN is required to file
Separate from existing Hyundai/Kia engine and theft settlements
proofDetails: VIN (Vehicle Identification Number) is required. No other documentation needed for the residual payment of $150-$350.
summary: Part of the $62.1M ZF-TRW airbag control unit settlement. Hyundai and Kia owners can claim $150 (unrecalled) to $350 (recalled) per vehicle. Just need your VIN - no other proof required for the residual payment.
caseInfo: In re: ZF-TRW Airbag Control Units Products Liability Litigation

title: $26M Lakeview Loan Servicing Data Breach Settlement
eligibility: Current or former customers of Lakeview Loan Servicing, Bayview Asset Management, Pingora Loan Servicing, or Community Loan Servicing whose data was compromised.
eligibilityBullets: Had a mortgage serviced by Lakeview, Bayview, Pingora, or Community Loan Servicing
Personal information was compromised in the data breach
Open claim form - no Notice ID required to start
proofDetails: Open claim form managed by Kroll. You can submit with name and address - no Notice ID required to start. Documentation needed for out-of-pocket loss reimbursement.
summary: Lakeview Loan Servicing agreed to pay $26M after a data breach compromised borrower information. One of the largest mortgage data breach settlements - open claim form managed by Kroll, no Notice ID needed to start filing.
caseInfo: Lakeview Loan Servicing Data Breach Litigation

title: Discover Card Merchant Interchange Fee Settlement
eligibility: Merchants who accepted Discover credit cards between 2007 and 2023 and were charged interchange fees on misclassified cards.
eligibilityBullets: Accepted Discover credit cards at your business
Between 2007 and 2023
Can register online even without a settlement notice
proofDetails: Register on the portal with your business information. Can register without a notice - the portal is open for new registrations.
summary: Merchants who accepted Discover credit cards can claim refunds for alleged overcharges on misclassified card interchange fees. You can register on the portal even if you didn't receive a notice.
caseInfo: Discover Card Merchant Interchange Fee Settlement

title: Whirlpool Refrigerator Wire Harness Defect Settlement
individualPayout: 30-50% of purchase price or free replacement door + labor
eligibility: Owners of Whirlpool-manufactured side-by-side refrigerators (2018-2021 models) from Whirlpool, Maytag, KitchenAid, or JennAir brands experiencing wire harness issues.
eligibilityBullets: Own a 2018-2021 Whirlpool, Maytag, KitchenAid, or JennAir side-by-side refrigerator
Experienced wire harness failure (broken wires affecting in-door ice/water)
Benefits scale by age: 50% of price (years 2-3), 30-40% (years 4-7)
proofDetails: Need to document the wire harness issue. Benefits include 30-50% of purchase price reimbursement or free replacement door with labor, depending on year of failure.
summary: If your Whirlpool, Maytag, KitchenAid, or JennAir side-by-side refrigerator (2018-2021) has broken wires affecting the in-door ice or water dispenser, you can get 30-50% of the purchase price back or a free replacement door.
caseInfo: Whirlpool Refrigerator Wire Harness Settlement

title: PHH Mortgage Insurance Kickback Settlement - $875 Per Loan
eligibility: Borrowers who had a PHH mortgage with private mortgage insurance where insurers entered reinsurance agreements with Atrium (PHH affiliate).
eligibilityBullets: Had a mortgage serviced by PHH Corporation
Were required to pay private mortgage insurance (PMI)
Must have received a settlement notice with ID and PIN
proofDetails: Need the Notice ID + PIN from your settlement notice to file online. No additional documentation needed - just the claim form.
summary: PHH Corp. agreed to settle claims it received illegal kickbacks on private mortgage insurance through its Atrium affiliate. Each qualifying borrower can claim $875 per loan. Final approval already granted - this is in the payment phase.
caseInfo: Munoz et al. v. PHH Corp. et al., E.D. California

title: $8.2M LastPass Data Breach Settlement - $25 to $10,000+
eligibility: U.S. residents whose LastPass accounts may have been compromised in the 2022 data breach.
eligibilityBullets: Had a LastPass account during the 2022 data breach
Paid users (Consumer Premium, Family, or Business) get $25 statutory payment
California residents get additional $100 CCPA payment
Must have Unique ID + PIN from email notice to file
proofDetails: Need your Unique ID + PIN from the email notice (sent starting late March 2026). The $25 statutory payment requires only an active paid account - no receipts. Documented losses up to $10,000.
summary: LastPass agreed to pay $8.2M cash (+ $16.25M crypto pool) after its 2022 data breach exposed user vaults. Paid subscribers can claim a $25 statutory payment with no documentation. California residents get an extra $100. Up to $10,000 for documented losses.
caseInfo: In re: LastPass Data Security Incident Litigation

title: $1M Avis Data Breach Settlement - Up to $5,000
eligibility: U.S. residents whose personal information was compromised in the Avis data breach between August 3-6, 2024.
eligibilityBullets: Personal info compromised in Avis Aug 2024 breach
Must have received a breach notification from Avis
Need Unique ID + PIN from notice to file online
proofDetails: Need Unique ID + PIN from your breach notice. Documented losses only - no flat cash option available. Up to $5,000 for identity theft or fraud expenses.
summary: Avis agreed to a $1.025M settlement after an August 2024 data breach compromised customer information. You need your Unique ID and PIN from the breach notice, plus documentation of any losses from identity theft or fraud.
caseInfo: In re: Avis Rent A Car System, LLC Security Incident Litigation

title: $2.6M Complete Payroll Solutions Data Breach Settlement
individualPayout: ~$100 pro rata or up to $5,000 documented
eligibility: Individuals whose personal information was potentially impacted by the Complete Payroll Solutions data breach.
eligibilityBullets: Employer used Complete Payroll Solutions for payroll processing
Personal information may have been compromised
Open form via Kroll - no Notice ID needed to start
proofDetails: Open claim form managed by Kroll - no Notice ID required. Can submit with name and address. Documentation needed for out-of-pocket losses.
summary: Complete Payroll Solutions agreed to pay $2.6M after a data breach compromised employee information. If your employer used CPS for payroll, your data may have been exposed. Open claim form - no Notice ID needed.
caseInfo: Complete Payroll Solutions Data Breach Settlement

title: $1.5M SouthState Bank Data Breach Settlement
individualPayout: Up to $3,500 documented or pro rata cash
eligibility: All persons who were sent notice that their personal information may have been impacted in the SouthState Bank data incident on or around February 7, 2024.
eligibilityBullets: Were sent a breach notice from SouthState Bank
Data incident occurred around February 7, 2024
Open form on Simpluris - no login gate
proofDetails: Open claim form on Simpluris - no Notice ID required. Up to $3,500 for documented losses, plus 1 year credit monitoring with $1M identity theft insurance.
summary: SouthState Bank agreed to pay $1.5M following a February 2024 data breach. Open claim form - no login required. Includes cash payments and 1 year credit monitoring with $1M identity theft insurance.
caseInfo: SouthState Bank Data Breach Settlement

title: $7.25M Patelco Credit Union Data Breach Settlement
individualPayout: $100-$200 pro rata or up to $5,000 documented
eligibility: Patelco Credit Union members whose personal information was stored on the platform accessed in the data incident (notified starting August 2024).
eligibilityBullets: Were a member of Patelco Credit Union
Data was on the platform accessed during the breach
Open form via Angeion - no Notice ID required
proofDetails: Open claim form managed by Angeion. Notice ID is optional ('if applicable'). Up to $5,000 for documented losses or $100-$200 pro rata cash payment.
summary: Patelco Credit Union agreed to pay $7.25M after a data breach. With ~500K members in California, this is one of the larger credit union breach settlements. Open claim form - Notice ID optional.
caseInfo: Patelco Credit Union Data Breach Settlement

title: Lee Enterprises Data Breach Settlement - $35 to $3,000
individualPayout: $35 alternative cash or up to $3,000 documented
eligibility: Individuals identified as being impacted by the Lee Enterprises data security incident, including those sent a notice.
eligibilityBullets: Lee Enterprises operates 75+ newspapers nationwide
If you were an employee, subscriber, or had data with any Lee paper
Open form on Simpluris - no login gate
proofDetails: Open claim form on Simpluris. $35 alternative cash with no documentation, or up to $3,000 for documented out-of-pocket losses. 1 year CyEx Financial Shield included.
summary: Lee Enterprises (owner of 75+ newspapers including the St. Louis Post-Dispatch, Buffalo News, and Omaha World-Herald) agreed to settle after a data breach. Open claim form for $35 cash or up to $3,000 for documented losses.

title: CSC ServiceWorks Data Breach Settlement - Up to $5,000
eligibility: Individuals whose personal information was compromised in the CSC ServiceWorks data security incident announced in August 2024.
eligibilityBullets: CSC ServiceWorks operates laundry machines in apartments nationwide
If you used their machines or were in their system
Need Claimant ID + last name from settlement notice
proofDetails: Need Claimant ID + last name from your mailed settlement notice. Up to $5,000 for documented losses plus $25/hr lost time (up to 4 hours). 2 years credit monitoring included.
summary: CSC ServiceWorks (the company behind laundry machines in many apartment buildings) agreed to settle after an August 2024 data breach. If you received a notice, you can claim up to $5,000 for documented losses plus credit monitoring.

individualPayout: ~$100 pro rata or up to $5,000 documented
eligibility: All persons in the United States who provided personal information to Anne Arundel Dermatology on or before December 9, 2025.
eligibilityBullets: Were a patient of Anne Arundel Dermatology
Broadest class definition - anyone whose data AAD held
Need Settlement Claim ID to file online
proofDetails: Need Settlement Claim ID from notice to file online. Up to $5,000 for documented losses or ~$100 pro rata cash. 3 years medical data monitoring included.
summary: Anne Arundel Dermatology agreed to pay $2.4M following a data breach. Broad class definition covers anyone whose personal data AAD held. Cash payments plus 3 years of medical data monitoring.
caseInfo: Anne Arundel Dermatology Data Breach Settlement

individualPayout: Up to $5,000 documented losses + alternative cash
eligibility: Individuals who received notice from Mizuno USA that their personal information was potentially compromised.

Need Notice ID from Atticus-managed settlement
Includes credit/identity theft protection
proofDetails: Need Notice ID from your settlement notice (Atticus administration). Documented losses up to $5,000 plus 2 years identity theft protection.
summary: Mizuno USA agreed to settle after a data breach compromised customer information. If you bought from Mizuno online and received a breach notice, you can file for cash payments plus 2 years of identity theft protection.

eligibility: Individuals who received a data breach letter from Signature Performance regarding their healthcare claims processing data.

Received a breach notification letter
Breach affected healthcare claims data
proofDetails: Must have received the breach notification letter. ~$200 estimated no-proof payment or up to $5,000 for documented identity theft losses.
summary: Signature Performance (a healthcare claims processor handling military/TRICARE data) agreed to pay $8.5M after a data breach. If you used TRICARE or military healthcare and received a breach notice, you can claim ~$200 or up to $5,000 for documented losses.

title: Ingram Micro Data Breach Settlement
eligibility: Individuals who received a breach notice from Ingram Micro regarding compromised personal information.
eligibilityBullets: Ingram Micro is a $50B+ IT distributor

Must have received breach notification
proofDetails: Must have received a breach notification. Cash payment amount depends on number of valid claims filed.
summary: Ingram Micro, one of the world's largest IT product distributors, agreed to settle after a data breach. If you were an employee, contractor, or business contact who received a breach notice, you can file a claim.

title: AbleTo Telehealth Robocall Settlement - $23
eligibility: Individuals who received prerecorded voicemails from AbleTo (telehealth company).
eligibilityBullets: Received unwanted prerecorded voicemails from AbleTo
Can look up eligibility by name or phone number
Or use Notice ID + PIN from mailed notice
proofDetails: File with Notice ID + PIN from your mailed notice, OR look up eligibility by name/phone number on the claim portal.
summary: AbleTo (a telehealth company) agreed to settle TCPA claims over unwanted prerecorded voicemails. Each valid claim gets $23. You can check eligibility by name or phone number on the portal.

TCPA violation claim

summary: Athena Bitcoin (operator of Bitcoin ATMs) agreed to settle TCPA claims over unwanted marketing calls and texts. Open claim form - you can file without a Notice ID.

eligibility: Job applicants who applied to positions at Splunk LLC in Washington state that lacked required pay range disclosures.

Job posting lacked required pay range info
Must have Unique ID + PIN from mailed notice
proofDetails: Need Unique ID + PIN from your mailed settlement notice to file online.
summary: Splunk (now Cisco) agreed to settle claims it violated Washington's Equal Pay and Opportunities Act by not including pay ranges in job postings. Each eligible applicant gets ~$1,674.

eligibility: Job applicants who applied to Tower Engineering positions in Washington state lacking required pay range disclosures.
eligibilityBullets: Applied for a job at Tower Engineering in WA
Job posting lacked required pay range info
Must have Unique ID + PIN from mailed notice
proofDetails: Need Unique ID + PIN from your mailed settlement notice.
summary: Tower Engineering agreed to settle WA Equal Pay and Opportunities Act claims for not disclosing pay ranges in job postings. Each applicant gets ~$1,259.

eligibility: Job applicants who applied to Beacon Sales positions in Washington state lacking required pay range disclosures.

Must have Unique ID + PIN from mailed notice
proofDetails: Need Unique ID + PIN from your mailed settlement notice.
summary: Beacon Sales agreed to settle WA Equal Pay and Opportunities Act claims. Each eligible WA job applicant gets ~$1,305.

eligibility: Job applicants who applied to Evergreen Alliance Golf (ClubCorp) positions in Washington state lacking required pay range disclosures.

Job posting lacked required pay range info
Must have Unique ID + PIN from mailed notice
proofDetails: Need Unique ID + PIN from your mailed settlement notice.

eligibility: Job applicants who applied to Veeam Management positions in Washington state lacking required pay range disclosures.

summary: Veeam Management agreed to settle WA Equal Pay and Opportunities Act claims for not disclosing pay ranges in job postings.

eligibility: Job applicants who applied to Ardagh Glass positions in Washington state lacking required pay range disclosures.
eligibilityBullets: Applied for a job at Ardagh Glass in WA
Job posting lacked required pay range info
Must have Unique ID + PIN from mailed notice
proofDetails: Need Unique ID + PIN from your mailed settlement notice.
summary: Ardagh Glass agreed to settle WA Equal Pay and Opportunities Act claims. Each eligible WA job applicant gets ~$1,315.

title: Travelers NJ Auto Insurance PIP Settlement - $70+
eligibility: New Jersey Travelers auto insurance policyholders with PIP (Personal Injury Protection) claims.
eligibilityBullets: Had Travelers auto insurance in New Jersey
Made a PIP claim
Auto $70 check mailed - file form by 6/16/26 for potentially much more
proofDetails: Auto $70 check mailed to all class members. File a claim by 6/16/26 for 80% of (PIP limit minus benefits paid minus $70) - potentially hundreds or thousands more. Need Unique ID/PIN from notice.
summary: Travelers agreed to settle NJ PIP insurance claims. You automatically get a $70 check, but filing a claim form by June 16 unlocks 80% of the gap between your PIP limit and benefits paid - potentially much more.

title: Leasing Expenses Co. NY AG Restitution
eligibility: Individuals or businesses who had leases from Leasing Expenses Co. or NLS-EF and were overcharged.
eligibilityBullets: Had a lease from Leasing Expenses Co. or NLS-EF
NY Attorney General restitution case
Submit claim online or return mailed form by 6/2/26
proofDetails: Complete and return the Claim Form that was mailed to you, or submit online by June 2, 2026.
summary: NY Attorney General restitution case against Leasing Expenses Co. for fraudulent leasing practices. If you had a lease and received a claim form, submit it by June 2 for restitution.
caseInfo: Leasing Expenses Co. NY AG Restitution

title: Zonolite Attic Insulation Trust - Up to $7,642
individualPayout: Up to $7,642 (paid at ~55% of allowed value)
eligibility: Homeowners with Zonolite-brand vermiculite attic insulation who paid for removal and re-insulation.
eligibilityBullets: Have Zonolite-brand vermiculite insulation in your attic
Paid for professional removal and re-insulation
Need original photos of the product + receipts for removal work
proofDetails: Need original photos proving the vermiculite is Zonolite brand (not PDFs), plus documented costs of removal and re-insulation. Max claim $7,642.50 paid at ~55%.
summary: The Zonolite Attic Insulation Trust reimburses homeowners who removed Zonolite-brand vermiculite insulation (which may contain asbestos). Up to $7,642 per claim. Trust operates through approximately 2034.
caseInfo: Zonolite Attic Insulation Trust (ZAI Trust)

title: Krispy Kreme Employee Data Breach Settlement - $75
eligibility: Current or former Krispy Kreme employees whose personal information was compromised in the November 29, 2024 data breach.
eligibilityBullets: Current or former Krispy Kreme EMPLOYEE (not customer)
Data compromised in Nov 29, 2024 breach (SSN, financial info)
Need Notice ID from postcard to file online
proofDetails: Need Notice ID from your postcard notice. $75 alternative cash payment requires no documentation. Up to $3,500 for documented out-of-pocket losses from the breach.
summary: Krispy Kreme agreed to settle after a November 2024 data breach exposed employee personal data including SSNs and financial info. EMPLOYEES ONLY (not customers). $75 cash with no docs, or up to $3,500 for documented losses.
caseInfo: In Re: Krispy Kreme Data Security Litigation

title: USC Student Late Fee Settlement
eligibility: Current or former USC students who were charged certain late fees between July 14, 2016 and March 4, 2026.
eligibilityBullets: Current or former USC student
Charged late fees between July 2016 and March 2026
Need Unique ID from settlement email to file
proofDetails: Need Unique ID from the settlement email. Cash payment plus late fee waiver and finance charge pause for all class members.
summary: USC agreed to settle claims over excessive late fees charged to students. If you're a current or former USC student who paid late fees since July 2016, you can claim a cash refund plus fee waiver.
caseInfo: Chaisson, et al. v. University of Southern California

eligibility: California residents who signed up for MUBI on or after April 1, 2021 with renewals between April 1, 2021 and May 21, 2025.

Signed up for MUBI streaming on/after April 1, 2021
Had subscription renewals between April 2021 and May 2025
Need Class Member ID from settlement notice
proofDetails: Need Class Member ID from the settlement notice to file online. Attestation under penalty of perjury - no purchase documentation needed.
summary: MUBI agreed to pay $1.6M to settle California auto-renewal claims. If you're a CA resident who subscribed to MUBI and had renewals between 2021-2025, you can claim an equal share (~$42 estimated).
caseInfo: Cejudo v. MUBI, Inc.

summary: Rebound Orthopedics agreed to pay $2.5M following a data breach. Patients can claim ~$75 in alternative cash or up to $5,000 for documented losses, plus 2 years of medical identity monitoring.

Patient data was compromised

eligibility: Individuals who were sent notice of the Outcomes One data incident.

Behavioral health company data breach
Deadline May 5 - file soon
proofDetails: Must have received the breach notification. Up to $3,500 for documented losses or undocumented cash payment from $1.7M fund.
summary: Outcomes One (behavioral health company) agreed to pay $1.7M after a data breach. If you received a breach notice, file by May 5 for up to $3,500 in documented losses or an undocumented cash payment.

eligibility: Individuals who received unwanted telemarketing calls or texts from Infiniti of Clarendon Hills (IL car dealership).
eligibilityBullets: Received unsolicited marketing calls/texts from Infiniti of Clarendon Hills
IL car dealership TCPA violation
Open claim form - no Notice ID required
proofDetails: Open claim form - provide your name, contact info, and confirm you received telemarketing from this dealership. One claim covers all calls/texts.
summary: Infiniti of Clarendon Hills (IL) agreed to pay $552,600 over unwanted telemarketing calls and texts. Each eligible claimant gets $150. Open claim form - no Notice ID needed.

eligibility: Boston Sports Club members who were charged during COVID closures or had difficulty canceling memberships.

Charged during COVID closures or had cancellation difficulties
Need Claim Number + PIN from email notice
proofDetails: Need Claim Number + PIN from email notice to select preferred payment method (Zelle or check).
summary: Boston Sports Club agreed to refund members charged during COVID closures or who had trouble canceling. Use your Claim Number and PIN from the email to select your payment method.

individualPayout: Replacement panels or prorated cash refund

Panels show delamination (layers separating)
Need photos of delamination + proof of ownership
proofDetails: Need photographs showing delamination plus proof of ownership of eligible Sanyo panels (BA2/BA3/BA5 series only). Email or mail claim form.
